title: Preparation of a holidic medium for Drosophila melanogaster
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"qzgrq","text":"We describe a novel holidic medium for Drosophila melanogaster that has been optimised for adult fitness traits. Development is also possible on this medium, albeit at a reduced rate. \r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ntnq1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fe3ti","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"077oz","text":"On this medium, adult egg laying, lifespan and activity patterns are no different from those of flies on a sugar yeast diet.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"qxvlf","text":"Drosophila melanogaster feeds on fermenting fruit. \r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1aumk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1kdt8","text":"\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9bnxx","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"wxktq","text":"Here we describe a synthetic, fully chemically defined (holidic) medium that it is sufficient for fruitfly development, albeit at a reduced rate. When adult flies are maintained on the diet, they are phenotypically similar to those kept on a natural yeast based (oligidic) diet. The holidic medium can produce more stable experimental outcomes than oligidic media, thus potentially improving inter-laboratory comparability. This medium also offers the opportunity to investigate the effects of subtle nutrient manipulations that could not be achieved otherwise.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"2vcen","text":"Reagents","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3dcag","text":"see table of reagents and order numbers in Table 1","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"frz8p","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"vse6f","text":"Equipment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2sj6e","text":"- silicone tubing\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qrrmd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3kv6m","text":"- autoclave\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nch58","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"tjntt","text":"- autoclave-resistant bottles\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dxtwi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fljuw","text":"- magnetic stirrer\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cyv0m","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4hgw5","text":"- sterile syringes\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ry5u","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"kjkuf","text":"- 0.22 micron syringe-mounted filters","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: purchase iungredients according to order sheet (Table 1)
prepare each of the stock solutions using the qauntities and according to the directions shown in Table 2, Table 3 and Table 4
Preparation of the medium is performed in two stages. (also see Table 5: example working recipes)
before autoclaving in a 1l, autoclave-proof, glass bottle, mark on the side the level of the target pre-autoclave volume (=1 litre minus the sum of the ingredients to be added after autoclaving)
add magnetic stirrer flea to bottle
to milliQ water, add sucrose and low solubility amino acids (L, I, Y)
add stock solutions for buffer base and metal ions
stir well (may not fully dissolve at this stage)
add cholesterol stock solution (solution will turn cloudy)
add agar
autoclave at 120 °C for 15 min (at the same time, autoclave dispensing tubing and any glass vials or bottles to house the medium)
allow solution to cool at room temperature with stirring to ~65 °C
add sterile stock solutions in order: amino acids, nucleosides/choline/inositol mix, vitamins and preservatives
using sterile tubing, dispense the solution into sterile vials
cover the vials and allow to cool for 90 min at room temperature
stored at 4 °C until use
MEDIUM DOESN'T SET: Try sterilising the buffer base separately from the medium and adding it after autoclaving.

Stock solution preparations: ~30 - 60min each medium preparation before autoclaving ~30 min autoclaving (variable depending on model) ~90 min medium mixing after autoclaving ~15 min dispensing medium ~30 min time to cool 90 min
none

A holidic medium for Drosophila melanogaster by Matthew D W Piper, Eric Blanc, Ricardo Leitão-Gonçalves, Mingyao Yang, Xiaoli He, Nancy J Linford, Matthew P Hoddinott, Corinna Hopfen, George A Soultoukis, Christine Niemeyer, Fiona Kerr, Scott D Pletcher, Carlos Ribeiro, and Linda Partridge published in Nature Methods on 18 Nov, 2013 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nmeth.2731

Stock_solutions_before_autoclaving.pdf Table 2 Stock solutions part 1 (those added before autoclaving) These stock solutions are to be made in advance of medium preparation. This batch are added to the medium before autoclaving. Stock_solutions_after_autoclaving.pdf Table 3 Stock solutions part 2 (added after autoclaving) These tables contain the stock solutions to be added after autoclaving. In most cases they are filter sterilised using a syringe-mounted filter. They are added after autoclaving to avoid nutritional loss upon heating. Stock_solutions_after_autoclaving_amino_acids.pdf Table 4 Stock solutions part 3 (added after autoclaving) These are amino acid stock solutions.Note, there are two tables here, each containing all 20 amino acids, but in differet ratios. The Yaa ratio is that most similar to what is found in yeast. example_working_recipes.pdf Table 5 example working recipe These recipes describe the ingredient list and quantities required to make up holidic media for HUNTaa and Yaa amino acid mixtures. Note, there are three recipes for each corresponding to different final amino acid levels in the media. Modifying these levels will alter adult fitness measures. reorder_list.pdf Table 1 Ordering list for holidic diet components A list of holidic medium components with order numbers. These are supplied in an attempt to standardise how people make the media and enhance replication of data between laboratories and trials.
